title: Missing module error in JavaScript code node
symptom: The JavaScript code node fails to execute, throwing an error indicating that the module ‘@n8n/di’ cannot be found. This occurs when running the workflow in a Kubernetes environment.
root_cause: The error occurs because the necessary module ‘@n8n/di’ is not present in the node_modules directory. This can happen if the Dockerfile used for building the image does not correctly copy all required dependencies, especially when transitioning from local development to a Kubernetes setup.
fix_steps: Modify the Dockerfile to ensure that all necessary dependencies are included.
Instead of deleting the entire node_modules directory, copy additional dependencies to a separate directory and set that as the path for node_modules.
agent_instruction: Update the Dockerfile to correctly handle dependencies for the JavaScript code node.
